findings.message: Hardcoded secret committed in source: `api_secret_key` defaults to the literal string "super-secret-key-do-not-share". Any value present in source control is no longer secret — it leaks via VCS history, container images, code search, and is identical across every deployment, enabling forgery/decryption of anything signed/encrypted with it. This also contradicts the class's own docstring ("Never hardcode config") and bypasses the `APP_` env/.env loading path the rest of the settings use.
findings.suggestion: Drop the literal default and source the secret from the environment with no fallback so the app fails fast if it is missing. For example: `api_secret_key: SecretStr` (required, no default) — `pydantic-settings` will then load it from `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` (or `.env` in dev) and raise on startup if unset. Provision the value via the deployment's secret store (and document it as a required secret in SECRETS.md); rotate the leaked value immediately and purge it from git history.
